title: Peloton
tagline: Video for a trucking safety company that helped raise a funding round
what:
Long before it was a bike in your bedroom, Peloton was two trucks on a highway. A Silicon Valley startup out of Stanford figured out how to use radar and ad-hoc wifi networks to link two semitrailers into a semi-autonomous "peloton" so they could draft behind each other at close range. The efficiency gains were worth the effort, with both trucks using less fuel if driving within 40' of each other, and those gains increasing as the gap closed to 10'. 

But Peloton had a challenge: they were an engineering company pitching investors on efficiency, while the rest of the world – and their drivers – just wanted to get home safe. We realized that a launch video pitching their product to future customers and investors would have to reframe their entire mission through the lens of safety. So we crafted an essentially wordless story, of father and son, to show the technology's affect on the humans behind the wheel. 

This video helped the company get onto Bloomberg News and raise their next round of funding. And sell the dream for safer freight to a generation of truckers.
why: Trucking technology is life-and-death but its audiences — fleet managers, investors, regulators — are not easily moved by specs. Video that makes the human and safety stakes vivid is more persuasive than any data sheet.
how: We shot on RED cinema cameras on highways across California. A drone team captured aerials. These are real trucks, real cars, real shots on real highways.
description: Long before the bike in your bedroom, Peloton was a truck on a highway
scraped_raw_text: Long before the bike in your bedroom, Peloton was a truck on a highway
This trucking technology company needed to emphasize safety to future customers and investors.
This video helped the company get onto Bloomberg News and raise their next round of funding. And sell the dream for safer freight to a generation of truckers.
